subject: Remove GPLv3 libraries & modules from Docker
commit_message:
Remove GPLv3 libraries & modules from Docker

I'm not happy how it is submitted:
copy-pasted generated stuff from upstream & edited to fit our needs...

We've asked docker guys if they would potentially support a GPLv3-free image,
but they do not have such plans.

Following GPLv3 libraries have been removed from build-deps:
- readline-dev
- coreutils
- findutils
- gdbm-dev

Which results in:
The necessary bits to build these optional modules were not found:
_dbm                  _gdbm                 readline

Issue-ID: INT-1933
Signed-off-by: Alexander Mazuruk <a.mazuruk@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I7f4d25ee9e282b5e2c89e8ccf083a789921cd369

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/integration-docker-onap-python-docker-verify-master/32/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/integration-docker-onap-python-docker-verify-master/32

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Can't you do this other way round, i.e. still use same base layer but just remove those 4 packages at the end of Dockerfile?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Can't you do this other way round, i.e. still use same base layer but just remove those 4 packages at the end of Dockerfile?

1. we need to do compliance for all the layers in containers. We would need separate base image or possibly squash the image during build (imho it should resolve legal issues but im not a lawyer)
2. those are build dependencies of python, all further build artifacts would need to be fixed. It is easier to just build it without those dependencies than maintaining a part with stripping those away post-build.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > Can't you do this other way round, i.e. still use same base layer but just remove those 4 packages at the end of Dockerfile?
> 
> 1. we need to do compliance for all the layers in containers. We would need separate base image or possibly squash the image during build (imho it should resolve legal issues but im not a lawyer)
> 2. those are build dependencies of python, all further build artifacts would need to be fixed. It is easier to just build it without those dependencies than maintaining a part with stripping those away post-build.

Ok thank you for detailed explanation. Yet still it will be harder to maintain versioning of this image - with each python base image Dockerfile update someone on our side will have to merge it with our Dockerfile.
Change has been successfully merged by Morgan Richomme
